markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Create fixtures covering Rust, TSX, JSON, migration SQL, malformed source, generated file exclusion, and stale symbol repair. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: CodeIndexFixtures leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CodeIndexFixtures is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 working tree: code-index implementation hardened after EventLedger JSONB idempotency false-conflict; real PostgreSQL tests green (knowledge_code_index_tests 22, knowledge_code_index:: lib 82, knowledge_code_nav_api_tests 2) plus MT-109 exact stress 25/25; adversarial re-review PASS 2026-06-14.

concerns: UserManual / model-manual coverage gap (spec 10.15.8): the 6 new model-callable nav routes (/knowledge/code/*) are NOT registered in src/model_manual/** (grep of model_manual for code_nav/knowledge_code/code-index -> zero hits) and knowledge_code_nav.rs has no model_manual hook. The delegate deferred this to MT-193. The routes are BUILT and callable now, so there is a window where a model-callable surface exists without the structured manual coverage 10.15.8 requires. The in-source mod.rs 'Model manual' docstring helps a human reader but is not the machine-readable manual surface a no-context model queries. Acceptable ONLY if MT-193 remains a tracked, committed obligation before these routes are exposed to models in production; flagging as a real (tracked) gap, not a silent omission.

Idempotency proof is shallow: mt104_reindex_is_idempotent_on_relationship_id asserts only edges1.len()==edges2.len() on a 2-function fixture. It does NOT assert evidence-span rows do not accumulate across re-index (create_knowledge_span re-inserts each run, see MT-104 concern), nor does it cover the entity-merge collisions (MT-098/099/100). The fixture coverage is happy-path; the collision and span-accumulation shapes are untested.

SKIP-on-no-PostgreSQL means CI without the managed PG proves NOTHING for 10 of 11 integration tests + the nav-API test (each returns early printing SKIP). The runtime guarantee depends on the gated PG being present; a green run in a PG-less environment is a false positive. Not a code defect, but the proof is conditional.
evasion_probes: NO SQLite / Docker / mock store: grep across src/knowledge_code_index/** + both test files for sqlite|docker|mock|in-memory -> zero (only the doc text 'no SQLite'/'never spawn'). The AppState pool and the engine handle point at the SAME isolated schema (knowledge_pg_support); tests assert against real knowledge_entities/spans/edges rows. The PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority constraint is honored.

mt112_indexes_mixed_tree_with_symbols_spans_edges, mt108 (partial failure), mt107 (staleness flip on content change), mt105 (scip ledger), mt104 (idempotent edges) each drive the real engine and re-query the DB to assert state - these are behavioral proofs, NOT tautologies. mt108 specifically proves a broken sibling does not abort the good file's indexing (DB query confirms rust:src/good.rs#ok present).

FAIL-CLOSED on PG-unavailable at PRODUCT level: the nav API's db.append_kernel_event / queries propagate StorageError -> storage_error -> 500 with no body; no fail-open path serves data when the store errors.

RUNTIME: re-ran cargo test --features runtime-full,test-utils --lib knowledge_code_index -> 43 passed/0 failed with the concurrent storage _v2 edit in place; integration+nav-API suite re-run launched against managed PG 127.0.0.1:5544 (PG-gated).
evidence_checked: src/backend/handshake_core/tests/knowledge_code_index_tests.rs (11 per-MT integration tests, real PG, no mocks)
grep sqlite|docker|mock across code-index + tests -> none
runtime: 43 lib tests green; integration suite PG-gated re-run in progress

hardening_closed.note: Closed for READY_FOR_VALIDATION. Prior HARDEN_RECOMMENDED concerns remain recorded in adversarial_code_review and are non-blocking follow-up.

evidence: EventLedger idempotency false-conflict root cause confirmed as PostgreSQL JSONB payload numeric normalization while canonical payload_hash matched.
append_kernel_event idempotency now compares canonical metadata plus payload_hash instead of raw JSONB Value equality; detectors accept enriched conflict diagnostics.
Adversarial re-review PASS: no blocking findings for MT-101/102/107/109/110/111/112 readiness.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-112 requires fixtures covering Rust, TSX, JSON, migration SQL, malformed source, generated-file exclusion, and stale-symbol repair, driving the real code-index engine into PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The committed tests deliver all of these against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL via the real CodeIndexEngine -> KnowledgeStore, reading the graph back to assert symbols/spans/edges. Real proof, spec-aligned (2.3.13.11), no placeholders or mocks.
findings: MT-112 (WP-KERNEL-009 CodeIndexFixtures) scope = fixtures covering Rust/TSX/JSON/migration-SQL/malformed/generated-exclusion/stale-repair; impl commit 5d6490b0+79e1a7e4. Note: unrelated MT-112s exist (WP-KERNEL-005 pose diagnostic, INF-9 subquadratic) and are out of scope here.

All named fixture families present in tests/knowledge_code_index_tests.rs: RUST_SRC (L204), TS_SRC (L231), TSX_SRC (L241, distinct .tsx with component+hook asserted L404-413), JS_SRC (L251), PKG_JSON (L257), JSON_SCHEMA (L262), MIGRATION_SQL (L271, incl. commented-out DDL that must NOT index, asserted L520-526), BROKEN_RUST (L284, malformed -> Partial parse_status asserted L370-374).

End-to-end test mt112_indexes_mixed_tree_with_symbols_spans_edges (L311-564) registers + indexes the 7-file mixed tree through the real engine.index_code_source and reads back real knowledge_entities/spans/edges; asserts entity_keys (L385-419), ast evidence span (L421-439), call edge with extractor_version/confidence/relationship_id (L441-456), and that every edge carries a source span ref citing spec 2.3.13.11 (L457-465).

Generated-file exclusion proven by mt112_ingestion_allowlist_excludes_generated_files_before_indexing (L566-634): allowlist exclude generated/** -> skipped_by_allowlist==1, generated/out.rs never becomes a knowledge source (L630-633).

Stale-symbol-repair coverage present: MT-107 staleness flip on content change (L1250+, StalenessVerdict::SourceChanged), MT-104 reindex replaces stale AST spans not accumulate (L1382), MT-108 binary read failure enqueues real repair queue entry reason READ_ERROR and resolves it (L1110-1239).

Real-resource proof confirmed: tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs uses managed_postgres::ManagedPostgres::ensure_running (port 5544) + PostgresDatabase::connect to an isolated knowledge schema; no SQLite/mock/in-memory. The only 'sqlite/mock' string hits in the test file are the docstring asserting their ABSENCE (L4).

Spec alignment: master-spec-v02.194 2.3.13.11 L2703 requires every edge to carry source span refs + extractor version + lifecycle + confidence + stable relationship_id; the MT-112 test asserts exactly this. Tests registered in Cargo.toml as [[test]] entries (L550-556).

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork only): tests SKIP loudly when PG binaries absent (noted by prior adversarial review as a CI-without-PG false-green risk); this is a conditional-proof caveat, not a code defect, and does not affect the fixtures' correctness when run against managed PG.
evidence_checked: git show 79e1a7e4 -- symbols.rs (impl-block entity_key collision fix)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
